value.body:
## Goal
Add Jira as a supported connector so users can authenticate with a Jira API key and make Jira-backed MCP capabilities available to chats and agentic workflows when the connector is enabled.

## Scope
- Add a Jira connector entry to the connectors UI/configuration flow.
- Allow users to provide and manage Jira authentication details, including API key/token and required Jira site/account metadata.
- Store credentials securely and avoid exposing secrets in logs, chat context, MCP responses, or agent traces.
- When the Jira connector is enabled and authenticated, expose its available MCP methods through Cycle MCP discovery.
- Allow chats and agentic workflows to detect that Jira is enabled and call the Jira-specific MCP methods.
- When Jira is disabled, unauthenticated, or misconfigured, omit or mark Jira methods unavailable with actionable error details.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can enable Jira from connectors and save valid Jira credentials.
- Cycle validates Jira credentials and reports connection/auth failures clearly.
- Cycle MCP can list available connectors and indicate whether Jira is enabled.
- Cycle MCP can list the MCP methods available for the Jira connector.
- Jira MCP methods are only available to chats and agentic workflows when the connector is enabled and authenticated.
- Disabled or invalid Jira configuration does not expose callable Jira MCP methods.
- Secrets are redacted everywhere outside secure credential storage.

## Notes
Initial Jira MCP methods can be limited to discovery and basic read operations if that keeps the first implementation small, but the connector contract should allow future Jira actions such as creating issues, linking issues, and updating statuses.

frontmatter.title: Add Jira connector support for MCP-enabled workflows

value.title: Add Jira connector support for MCP-enabled workflows
